comment: Reminiscent of an orange influenced Caipirinha.

name: Caneflower cocktail
ingredients: ¾ shot Aperol Aperitivo by Fratelli Barbieri
¾ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Rust coloured and bittersweet with cachaça and elderflower.

name: Orinoco cocktail
ingredients: ¼ shot Creme de cacao liqueur (white)
⅓ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Dry sherry, chocolate and coconut add subtle nuances to a base of cachaça and apple juice.

name: New Orleans Minute cocktail
ingredients: ¼ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
1⅔ shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
⅔ shot Carpano Antica Formula Vermouth
½ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
⅛ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: POUR mezcal into chilled glass and swirl to coat. Discard excess mezcal. STIR other ingredients with ice and strain into mezcal rinsed glass.
garnish: Lemon and orange zests (discarded) & 3 coffee beans floated on drink
comment: Perhaps not a Bourbon Street staple but characterful nonetheless with tequila, mezcal, aromatised wine, maraschino and orange bitters.

name: Violet Negroni cocktail
ingredients: 2 shot Byrrh Grand Quinquina Aromatized Wine
½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and STIR.
comment: Bittersweet with a strong hint of gentian. A great aperitif.

name: Bitches' Brew cocktail (D's recipe)
ingredients: 1 shot Byrrh Grand Quinquina Aromatized Wine
¾ shot Rutte Old Simon Genever
¾ shot India pale ale (IPA) beer
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
garnish: Orange zest twist and Luxardo maraschino cherry
comment: Beer has never tasted so complex - sweet aperitif, dry hoppy IPA beer, bready jenever and cleansing vodka.

name: Chanbanger cocktail
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Ocho Anos Gran Reserva Rum
⅔ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
⅓ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
¾ shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Like its creator this drink is hard to classify. A hint of Singapore Sling oriental cherry with some Cuban Daiquiri aged rum and a splash of Papa’s grapefruit citrus sourness.

name: Pastry War Margarita
ingredients: 1 shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
½ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
½ shot Montenegro Amaro Premiata Specialita
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Mezcal, amaro, elderflower liqueur and salt add attitude and complexity to this Margarita-styled cocktail.

name: Chutes & Ladders cocktail
ingredients: 1¾ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
1 shot Carlshamns Flaggpunsch Original Swedish Punsch
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Citrusy tequila-led with Swedish punch and Cocchi Americano rounding and adding their distinctive flavours.

name: Edison's Medicine cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot AquaRiva Organic Agave Syrup
½ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Cachaça leads with basil and Chartreuse herbal notes, zesty lime and agave richness.

name: Roman Highball
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
Top up with Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with soda.
comment: A refreshing Pimm’s Cup-like. A great long drink to come home to.

name: Wet cocktail
ingredients: ⅓ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into glass filled with crushed ice.
comment: Superbly refreshing. The pinch of cinnamon adds delicate spice which combines well with cachaça and ginger ale.

name: Spanish Eyes
ingredients: ¾ shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
¾ shot Byrrh Grand Quinquina Aromatized Wine
¾ shot Zoco Pacharan Navarro Liqueur
⅛ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
¾ shot Freshly squeezed orange juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Reminiscent of a Mexican Blood & Sand, refreshing and complex.

name: Claudine cocktail
ingredients: ⅓ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
⅓ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
⅓ shot Byrrh Grand Quinquina Aromatized Wine
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Dry and aromatic, the combination of Byrrh and armagnac reveals interesting favours of dried apricot and chocolate.

name: To Be Oranged cocktail
ingredients: 1¼ shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
1¼ shot Byrrh Grand Quinquina Aromatized Wine
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Orange zest (discarded) & orange wedge on rim
comment: To be arranged - In the meantime shake your tangerine with this pleasing earthy tequila and zesty fruity number.

name: Greek Celery Cocktail
ingredients: ⅔ shot Freshly extracted celery juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Celery and mastiha pair well, while gin adds a spirituous bite and botanical complexity. A small grind of pepper to finish adds a nuance of spice.

name: Caipirissima
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: MUDDLE lime wedges in the base of shaker to release juice and oils in its skin. Add rum and sugar. SHAKE with 6oz scoop crushed ice and pour all without straining into glass.
comment: A Daiquiri-like drink made in the style of a Caipirinha.

name: Gun Metal Blue cocktail
ingredients: 1½ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
½ shot Lejay Curacao Bleu (blue curacao) Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
¼ shot Crème de pêche (peach) liqueur
¾ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Orange, peach and cinnamon subtly flavour this mezcal based margarita.

name: Greek Martini
ingredients: ¾ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Mastiha (which after all is botanically flavoured spirit) combines wonderfully with gin and dry vermouth. A splash of ouzo adds more Greek interest.

name: Aristotle cocktail
instructions: Lightly MUDDLE mint in base of stirring glass. Add other ingredients, STIR with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: The thinking man’s mixed drink, this is based on gin with the distinctive flavour of Greek mastiha and lengthened with birch water.

name: Woody cocktail
instructions: MUDDLE mint in base of stirring glass. Add other ingredients, STIR with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Named not after the cheery chap in Cheers or even the Beatles’ Norwegian Wood but due to this particularly Woody cocktail containing birch water and mastiha.

name: South of Heaven cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Fernet Branca digestive bitter liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Bittersweet and refreshing with the very distinctive flavours of mastiha and Fernet.

name: Four Skin Daiquiri
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Daiquiris are refreshing but this mastiha influenced example is particularly so. As ‘four’ the name, well it’s a Daiquiri with four ingredients, including mastiha and the brand we used was Skinos, so appropriate, we think you’ll agree.

name: Smoky Tears cocktail
ingredients: 1½ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
¾ shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
⅛ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Best described as being a mezcal, mastiha and pink grapefruit Margarita. It’s a great combo. Mastiha freshness with a hint of smoky mezcal is balanced by slightly acidic pink grapefruit juice.

name: Fresh White Lady cocktail
ingredients: ¾ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE without ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Mastiha and peppermint bitters bring minty freshness to this riff on the classic White Lady.

name: Minty Pentones cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Giffard Menthe Pastille white crème de menthe liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into glass filled with crushed ice.
comment: Gin’s botanical complexity combined with minty and mastiha freshness and a pink hint of cranberry fruitiness makes for a cleansing after dinner digestive.

name: Malecon Cocktail
ingredients: 1⅔ shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
1 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: float chunk of block ice (or ice cube)
comment: Best described as a fortified wine rich Daiquiri.

name: Tommi's Cocktail
ingredients: 1½ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
1 shot Aperol Aperitivo by Fratelli Barbieri
½ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Orange zest twist (discarded) & Luxardo maraschino cherry
comment: A delicately bittersweet aperitif cocktail which heros complex smoky mezcal.

name: Perfectly Paired cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Lightly spiced vanilla liqueur
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass. TOP with champagne.
garnish: Dust with cinnamon & pear slice on rim
comment: This autumnal champagne cocktail has notes of tart orchard fruit with vanilla and cinnamon laced with grassy cachaça.

name: Apple Black Royal
ingredients: ½ shot Lejay Creme de Mure (Blackberry) Liqueur
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass. TOP with champagne.
comment: Richly flavoured apple and blackberry fruit liqueurs, bolstered and fortified with calvados, charged and given character with rose champagne.

name: Thuir Sour
ingredients: 1 shot Byrrh Grand Quinquina Aromatized Wine
½ shot De Kuyper Apricot Brandy Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
¾ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Perfectly balanced sweet and sour apricot with faint red wine notes.

name: Copenhagen Cocktail
ingredients: ⅔ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
⅔ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Bready notes in the jenever combine with the cherry brandy and the lime juice to produce a complex bittersweet, jammy cocktail.

name: Dried Meadow Flower
ingredients: 1½ shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
Top up with Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
instructions: SHAKE first 4 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Floral elderflower and tequila with a hint of bittersweet gentian and refreshing lemon.

name: Sunny Disposition cocktail
ingredients: 2 shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
½ shot De Kuyper Apricot Brandy Liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅛ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
1 dash Dr. Adam Elmegirab's Boker's Bitters
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Slightly on the bitter side of bittersweet with gentian, jammy apricot and lemon juice over a tequila base.

name: Zuzu's Petals cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Bittersweet with fragrant bison grass vodka and fruity maraschino cherry flavours supported by bitter gentian, rich elderflower and lime juice.

name: Reggatta de Blanc
ingredients: 1 shot BarSol Supremo Mosto Verde Italia Pisco
½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
⅛ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment:
Dry and boozy.

“White Reggae” was the second, and seminal, album by the English rock band The Police. The album's French name reflects the heavy influence of the country’s products in this cocktail. Happily for approval by our naming committee (my missus), most of the ingredients are also decidedly “Blanc”.

name: Reverse Vesper Martini
ingredients: 2 shot Lillet Blanc French aperitif wine
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: More a subtly fortified wine than a true “three and under the guest” Martini but delightfully so with sherry-like wine notes and faint botanical influences. Subtly refined. Despite the ‘Vesper’ name some will say that this should be stirred. Shake boldly on, but with good ice.

name: Alexander The Great
ingredients: ½ shot Creme de cacao liqueur (white)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A tasty combination of coffee, chocolate and cream, laced with vodka.

name: Call Me Old-Fashioned
ingredients: ¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: STIR sugar syrup and bitters with two ice cubes in a glass. Add 1½ shots of cognac and two more ice cubes. STIR some more and add another two ice cubes and another shot of cognac. Stir lots more and add more ice.
comment: An Old-Fashioned made with cognac instead of whiskey. Works well.

name: Andean Dusk cocktail
ingredients: 1 shot BarSol Supremo Mosto Verde Italia Pisco
½ fresh Freshly squeezed lemon juice
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: MUDDLE grapes in base of shaker. Add next 3 ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass. TOP with champagne.
garnish: Drop cold red grape into drink
comment: Dry biscuity champagne adds sparkle to sweet red grapes while pisco fortifies and adds complex distilled grape flavours.

name: Minty White Lady cocktail
ingredients: ¾ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
¾ shot Giffard Menthe Pastille white crème de menthe liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and strain into chilled glass.
comment: White crème de menthe and peppermint bitters bring minty freshness to this not so innocent lady in white.

name: Sailor's Swizzle
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Carta Oro Superior Gold Rum
⅛ shot St. Elizabeth Allspice Dram
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: POUR ingredients into chilled glass and two-thirds fill with crushed ice. SWIZZLE with a swizzle stick (or churn with a barspoon). Add more crushed ice to fill and SWIZZLE some more. Serve with straws.
garnish: A few dashes Angostura bitters over ice cap crowned with lime zest strings
comment: Port wine is further fortified with golden rum and delicately spiced with Pimento Dram in this long swizzled punch.

name: Miss Moonshine
ingredients: ⅔ shot Lillet Blanc French aperitif wine
⅓ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Gentian, elderflower and rosemary combine harmoniously, over a Poitín base with aromatised wine.

name: Boozy Suzie
ingredients: ½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
¼ shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
2 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Pink grapefruit zest twist (discarded) & 3 spots of Peychaud’s bitters drawn through foamy head
comment: Bittersweet gentian is the prevalent flavour (hence the 'Suzie' name), toned down by bianco vermouth and freshened with pink grapefruit over a fortifying grainy vodka base. Peychaud’s subtly adds its distinctive flavour.

name: Calvados Cocktail
ingredients: ¾ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Tangy orange with an alcoholic apple bite.

name: Spicy Fifty
ingredients: 2 slice Jalapeño or Fresno chilli pepper (fine sliced)
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: MUDDLE chilli in base of SHAKER. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Fresh chilli on rim (warn drinker not to eat)
comment: Chilli spice smoothed by honey with vanilla vodka and lime juice.

name: Salvatore Meets
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE first 4 ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass. TOP with champagne.
comment: A refreshing zesty lemon Daiquiri topped with a splash of champagne.

name: Ironbound
ingredients: 1 shot Laird's Straight Bottled in Bond applejack
⅛ shot Demerara sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
2 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Adapted from a drink created by Mayur Subbarao of New York, USA.

name: Pearl Button
ingredients: ¾ shot Lillet Blanc French aperitif wine
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with lemonade.
comment: Cachaça, Lillet Blanc and citrus combine in this dry, refreshing drink.

name: Ambrosio Cup
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
1 shot Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
instructions: SHAKE first 6 ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass. TOP with soda water.
comment: Cachaça, Bison Grass vodka and manzanilla sherry combine harmoniously and are freshened with mint and lime in this refreshing cocktail.

name: Picotin
ingredients: ¾ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ shot Aperitivo Cocchi Americano Bianco
⅓ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled glass.
comment: Bright yellow and on the bitter side of bittersweet with aquavit, lemon, gentian and maraschino liqueurs.

name: Sakura Martini
ingredients: ¼ spoon Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: This Reverse Martini-style drink features a floating salted cherry blossom as a garnish. This is not just for looks, the hint of saltiness it lends this delicate drink replaces that which an olive typically brings to a Martini.

name: The Cornichon
ingredients: 1½ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
¼ shot Grand Marnier Cordon Rouge Liqueur
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
¼ shot Menjurje de Ancho Reyes Chile liqueur
½ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: A cornucopia of flavours with which to get pickled.

name: Queen's Park Hotel Super Cocktail
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Carta Oro Superior Gold Rum
½ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Giffard Sirop Grenadine (Grenadine Syrup)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Perhaps best described as being a Trinidadian Daiquiri due to its heavy use of Angostura Bitters. Obviously it would also have originally been made with Trinidad gold rum, Canning's Caroni Rum according to Owen Rutter. Preferably make your own pomegranate (grenadine) syrup.

name: The Angel Wore Red
ingredients: ⅓ shot Giffard Menthe Pastille white crème de menthe liqueur
instructions: STIR first 3 ingredients with ice. POUR beer into ice-filled glass and then strain stirred ingredients into glass and lightly stir.
comment: A most unusual combination of classic Italian red bitter liqueur, bianco vermouth, crème de menthe and lager combine brilliantly in this refreshing aperitif.

name: Mr Simon
ingredients: ⅔ shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Your choice of jenever and orange curaçao greatly effects the balance of this cocktail. Salvatore uses Rutte Old Simon which is not only a fantastic jenever with an attractive nutty character but also befits the name. The choice of a drier, high alcohol curaçao obviously produces a correspondingly drier drink.

name: Eastside Gimlet
ingredients: ¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
½ shot Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
instructions: MUDDLE cucumber in base of shaker. Add gin, lime juice and sugar syrup, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass. TOP with a splash of soda.
comment: A riff on the Southside using cucumber – an ingredient originally inspired by Hendrick’s Gin having recently launched when this gin cocktail was first created.

name: Waterloo
ingredients: ½ shot Mandarine Napoleon Grande Liqueur Imperiale
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Orange zest twist (discarded) & float pickled almond
comment:
Created by yours truly (Simon D) at the Cabinet Room, London, England.

Named after the Battle of Waterloo which was fought on Sunday 18th June 1815 near the town of Waterloo in present-day Belgium, then part of the Netherlands. The French army commanded by Napoleon Bonaparte was defeated by an Anglo-allied army commanded by the Duke of Wellington fittingly alongside a Prussian army commanded by Gebhard Leberecht von Blücher, Prince of Wahlstatt. Napoleon's defeat at Waterloo brought his reign as French Emperor to a sudden end.

name: Division Bell
ingredients: 1 shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
¾ shot Aperol Aperitivo by Fratelli Barbieri
½ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: A mezcal inspired variation of the Last Word with a refreshing, boozy blend of ingredients.

name: Joy Division
ingredients: ½ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
3 dash La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: A 2:1 Dry Martini with slug of triple sec and dashes of absinthe contributing zesty orange and subtle aniseed respectively.

name: Juliet & Romeo
ingredients: ¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: MUDDLE cucumber in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Float 3 drops rose water, 3 drops Angostura bitters & mint leaf
comment: You smell rose water but taste gin, lime, cucumber and mint. A refreshing summery cocktail.

name: Moon Cocktail
ingredients: ⅙ shot Crème de pêche (peach) liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Gin and sherry combine harmoniously in this stirred boozy cocktail with a  honeyed touch of jammy peach.

name: The Affiliate
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Ocho Anos Gran Reserva Rum
¾ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Judicious dashing of orange bitters and hitting the correct dilution will make or break this drink, which is a fine balance between aged rum and rich cherry liqueur versus dry fino sherry and aromatic bitters.

name: Alcatraz
ingredients: ½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
¼ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Dry and lightly bitter, this tequila-based sipper makes for a great aperitif cocktail.

name: The Bennett Cocktail
ingredients: ¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled glass.
comment: Judicious dashing of bitters makes or breaks this riff on the classic Gimlet.

name: Bijou Cocktail (inspired by Lawor's 1895 recipe)
ingredients: ¾ shot Grand Marnier Cordon Rouge Liqueur
¾ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment:
A boozy sipper of a cocktail that’s medium dry with honeyed richness. Gin based with orange zesty freshness and sweet vermouth adding deep herbal complexity. A great after-dinner cocktail that also works well on-the-rocks.
AKA: Not to be confused with the more commonplace equal parts gin, Chartreuse and vermouth Bijou which first appeared in Harry Johnson's 1900 Bartender's Manual.

name: Mediocre
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%) (infused with vanilla)
¼ shot Honey water (3 honey to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Ripe pineapple, rich vanilla and honey laced with rum. The use of mead in this drink inspired the ‘Mediocre’ name.

name: Canary Flip
ingredients: ¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A delightful balance of egg, spirit and wine.

name: Pinecone
ingredients: 1½ shot BarSol Supremo Mosto Verde Italia Pisco
½ shot Lightly spiced vanilla liqueur
instructions: MUDDLE cloves in base of shaker to crush. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Honey and pineapple, laced with aromatic pisco and spiced with clove.

name: Well-fortified
ingredients: ¾ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: A Bamboo meets a Vodka Martini in this boozy, vodka-laced aromatic aperitif cocktail, so named due to this cocktail packing a trio of fortified wines.

name: Carré Nouveau
ingredients: ¾ shot Lillet Blanc French aperitif wine
⅙ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
3 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Reminiscent of a Vieux Carré but with Lillet Blanc replacing sweet vermouth and gentian replacing Angostura bitters.

name: Tribute to Martell
ingredients: ⅙ shot Créme de violette liqueur
instructions: SPRAY violet liqueur onto inside surface of glass and set aside. STIR cognac with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Originally made with Martell Cordon Bleu, this sophisticated champagne cocktail has delicate floral notes.

name: The Savelberg
ingredients: ⅓ shot Poire William eau de vie
½ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
⅙ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Boozy with bready genever, pear, citrus and faint rich honey.

name: Trade Winds
ingredients: ⅔ shot Carpano Antica Formula Vermouth
⅙ shot Gabriel Boudier Dark Creme de Cacao Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Coconut water adds dilution and subtly flavours this cognac-based cocktail with sweet vermouth and subtle notes of chocolate and Angostura.

name: Je suis l'Amour
ingredients: ⅔ shot Martini Ambrato Riserva Speciale
½ shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
instructions: THROW all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Cognac and cask aged dry vermouth form the base of this refined cocktail with very subtle ginger, pink grapefruit and chocolate notes.

name: Élysée Treaty
ingredients: ⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Unbelievably this zesty short cocktail contains a whacking 2/3 of Jägermeister which combines harmoniously with the cognac.

name: Indimenticabili Momenti (Unforgettable Moments)
ingredients: ⅙ shot Liquore Strega Giuseppe Alberti
⅙ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Slightly sweet with vanilla, cherry and cinnamon and complex peppermint and herbal notes from the Strega interlaced with cognac.

name: The Cab
ingredients: ⅚ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE first 5 ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass. TOP champagne.
comment: A minty limey cognac sour invigorated with champagne.

name: Mon Dernier Mot (My Last Word)
ingredients: ⅔ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Tangy lemon and cognac with sweet herbal and ginger notes. An interesting riff on The Last Word.

name: Banana Split
ingredients: ¾ shot Giffard Banane du Brésil crème de banane liqueur
instructions: STIR first 5 ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass. Shake sealed container of cream and carefully POUR over the back of a spoon to form a small white spot floating in the centre of the drink.
garnish: Lightly dust surface of drink with chocolate powder & banana chunk on rim
comment: A banana flavoured after-dinner sipper of cocktail with coconut water subtly flavouring and smoothing cognac.

name: Art de Vivre (Art of Living)
ingredients: ⅙ shot Fernet Branca digestive bitter liqueur
⅙ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Cognac freshened and flavoured with Mastiha, Fernet Branca and chocolate bitters.

name: La Garde de Nuit (The Night's Watch)
ingredients: ⅙ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Cognac with red berry and red wine notes with faint honeycomb.

name: Lovelight
ingredients: ⅓ shot Carpano Antica Formula Vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain in ice-filled glass.
comment: Reminiscent of a cognac-based Negroni with a hint of cinnamon spice.

name: Challenger Deep
ingredients: 1 shot Cantine Gancia Antica Ricetta Barolo Chinato
¼ shot Chartreuse Verte (Green Chartreuse) Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: This aperitif style cocktail has chocolatey notes with faint rich herbal complexity from the Chartreuse.

name: L'Indochine
ingredients: ⅓ shot Lapsang Souchong Tea liqueur
instructions: STIR all the ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: This stirred cognac-based cocktail is reminiscent both an old-fashioned and a Godfather with notes of citrus, coffee and tea.

name: Celebration
ingredients: 1 shot Martini Riserva Speciale Rubino Vermouth di Torino
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Reminiscent of a cognac-based Negroni served straight-up.

name: Parmenides
ingredients: ⅓ shot Chartreuse Verte (Green Chartreuse) Liqueur
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅔ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
4 dash La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: A sour-style drink served straight-up combining cognac, Green Chartreuse, white wine and lemon.

name: Rock of Sages
instructions: MUDDLE cherries in base of shaker. Add sage leaves and lightly bruise with the muddler. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: As the name suggests, sage subtly influences this fruity cognac based cocktail served on the rocks. Fruity and easy.

name: The Comeback
ingredients: ½ shot De Kuyper Apricot Brandy Liqueur
⅙ shot Fernet Branca digestive bitter liqueur
¼ shot Maple shrub (1:1.2 cider vinegar to maple syrup)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: A complex cocktail with apricot fruit to the forefront and underlying Fernet Branca bitterness.

name: A Rose By Any Other Name
ingredients: ⅚ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
⅙ shot Créme de violette liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
garnish: Turkish Delight & grate dark chocolate over drink
comment: Best made using Martell Cordon Blue as per the original recipe, this is a dry boozy sipper with faint floral and chocolate notes. Perhaps forgo the grated chocolate garnish in favour of a square of dark chocolate balanced on Turkish Delight.

name: Revolutionary Flip
ingredients: ½ shot Warre's Otima 10 Year Old Tawny Port
⅓ shot Demerara sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE without ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Full-bodied both describes this drink and how you’ll feel after consuming this very tasty meal of a drink.

name: DaVinci Code
ingredients: ½ shot Warre's Otima 10 Year Old Tawny Port
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Coffee and salted caramel stirred with cognac and a splash of tawny port.

name: The French Canadian
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: MUDDLE cucumber in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Dry and aromatic with cucumber adding freshness. A good aperitif cocktail.

name: C'est la Vie (That's Life)
ingredients: ⅓ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A tasty apple and cognac sour with delicate herbal and cinnamon spice.

name: The Devil In Me
ingredients: ⅓ shot Menjurje de Ancho Reyes Chile liqueur
½ shot Akashi-Tai Shiraume Ginjo Umeshu
⅓ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Plum sake, chilli spice and maraschino cherry delicately flavour cognac in this stirred, boozy after dinner cocktail.

name: Excuse My French
ingredients: ⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
½ shot Lejay Creme de Framboise (raspberry) Liqueur
1 spoon Saline solution 10:1 (5g sea salt to 50ml water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Lemon zest encircling the inside rim of glass with crusted sugar coated rim.
comment: Raspberry flavours characterise this Martell VSOP Medallion cognac sour served crusta-style.

name: Le Commercant (The Merchant)
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
2 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
Top up with Martini Prosecco D.O.C.
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: A balance between citrus and berry sweetness forms the heart of this cognac based cocktail.

name: Good Afternoon
ingredients: ⅔ shot Rutte Old Simon Genever
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled tea cup.
comment: Created in 2015 by Roberto Conti at Mojito Café, Italy.

name: Late Night
ingredients: ¾ shot Poire William pear liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Dust with grated tonka beans & dried rose petals
comment: This boozy stirred drink hits a perfect balance between sweet fruity pear, rich oloroso sherry and fortifying cognac.

name: Butler's Tipple
ingredients: ½ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
⅓ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Almost creamy with vanilla, bittersweet rootiness and cognac.

name: The Continental Exchange
ingredients: ⅔ shot Akashi-Tai Shiraume Ginjo Umeshu
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Long and refreshing with subtle flavours of apple, lemon, ginger and plum wine combining well with the cognac base.

name: Thyme for…
ingredients: 3 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
½ shot Grand Marnier Cordon Rouge Liqueur
⅓ shot Carpano Antica Formula Vermouth
instructions: DASH bitters over sugar cube in stirring glass and MUDDLE cube to crush. ADD other ingredients and stir to dissolve sugar. Add ice and STIR. Fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: It most certainly is! As the name suggests, aromatic thyme subtly influences this spirituous cognac-based cocktail, with Grand Marnier adding rich but delicate zesty orange notes.

name: The Beloved
ingredients: ⅓ shot Giffard Banane du Brésil crème de banane liqueur
1 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
1 drop Saline solution 10:1 (5g sea salt to 50ml water)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: A wonderfully complex marriage of cognac, distilled chocolate, rich Pedro Ximénez and banana liqueur with aromatic bitters and a dash of brine.

name: B&B Royale
instructions: STIR first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass. TOP with champagne.
comment: A crowd pleasing cognac-based cocktail – will appeal to the many and upset few – with the monastic herbal complexity of Bénédictine with rich black raspberry, topped and effervesced with champagne.

name: Three Hundred Poems
ingredients: ½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Blackcurrant jam adds rich concentrated berry notes to this attractive crimson red cognac laced cocktail.

name: Titre Provisoire (Provisional Title)
ingredients: ⅕ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Characterful cognac, herbal Bénédictine and rich fig liqueur are harmoniously balanced by dry vermouth and cider vinegar. This drink is finely balanced but when achieved it sings.

name: French Kiss #3
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
instructions: MUDDLE raspberries in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Raspberry and rich almond tamed by lime with underlying cognac character.

name: Terroir
ingredients: ½ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Chablis’ white wine acidity balances rich nettle cordial in this sweet ‘n’ sour cognac based cocktail.

name: Repose' 1912
ingredients: ¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
⅛ shot Balsamic vinegar of Modena
instructions: STIR first 5 ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass. Top with champagne.
comment: Cognac with sharp balsamic notes and hints of chocolate, enlivened with champagne.

name: Mart-Inez
ingredients: ⅔ shot Rutte Old Simon Genever
½ shot Aperitivo Cocchi Americano Bianco
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled glass.
comment: Grape and grain combine harmoniously in this sipping cocktail.

name: Carnaghino
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass (preferably block ice).
comment: Cognac, luxurious chocolate and pistachio. What’s not to like?

name: Le Coq d'Or (The Golden Rooster)
ingredients: 3 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
½ shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
2 dash Dale DeGroff's Pimento Aromatic Bitters
instructions: Saturate sugar cube with Peychaud's in base of shaker and MUDDLE thoroughly. Add cognac and stir to ensure sugar is dissolved. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Grapefruit zest twist (discarded) & float star anise
comment: Perhaps substitute ¼ shot Demerara sugar syrup for the sugar cube and all that muddling. Whether runny or dry sugar, this flip style cocktail is balanced and complex.

name: Don Pone
ingredients: ⅙ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
½ shot Creme de cacao liqueur (white)
instructions: RINSE glass with maraschino liqueur. STIR other ingredients with ice and strain into maraschino rinsed glass.
garnish: Crystallised ginger on stick & grated dark chocolate
comment: Maraschino cherry aromas strike you first as you enjoy this cognac-laced, subtly chocolate and ginger after dinner cocktail.

name: L'Heritage
ingredients: 1½ shot Lillet Blanc French aperitif wine
⅓ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
⅙ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
2 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Orange zest twist (discarded) & Luxardo maraschino cherry
comment: Also try this aperitif-style bittersweet cocktail served on-the-rocks.

name: Triomphe d'Anis (Triumph of Aniseed)
ingredients: ⅙ shot La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: RINSE glass with absinthe. STIR other ingredients with ice and strain into absinthe rinsed glass.
garnish: Orange zest twist (discarded) & float star anise
comment: A boozy stirred sipper, perhaps after-dinner cognac-based cocktail, with hints of anise coffee and dry sherry complexity.

name: The Good, The Bad and the Ugni
ingredients: 1 shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
⅓ shot Chartreuse Verte (Green Chartreuse) Liqueur
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass (preferably block of ice). Lastly, FLOAT Chartreuse.
comment: Perhaps it’s the holier-than-thou Chartreuse that is playing the part of the bad guy in this tasty drink. The cognac (made with Ugni Blanc grapes) play the strong, characterful part.

name: White Old Fashioned
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
⅓ shot Clément Sirop Sucre de Canne (sugar cane juice syrup)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Joerg makes this cocktail using Bacardi Neo but it also works well with the easier to obtain Bacardi Carta Blanca. If you haven't any Sexy Bitters then try with a dash or orange bitters.

name: Gin Old Fashioned
ingredients: ⅙ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: A couple of shots of bready genever with a spoon of sugar and a couple of dashes of bitters - incredibly simple, equally classic, and with the right genever, incredibly tasty.

name: Velasco and Sand
ingredients: 1 shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
¾ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
¾ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Mezcal adds a faintly smoky note to this riff on the classic Blood & Sand. The proportion of spirit and orange juice is slightly raised from the usual equal parts formula – the extra nip of mezcal adds an uplifting kick to the drink while more orange adds zippy freshness and lightens what can otherwise be a heavy cocktail.

name: Smoke & Mirrors (by The Bon Vivants)
ingredients: ½ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into glass filled with crushed ice.
comment: Genever and mezcal sit surprisingly well together in this very refreshing drink with mint and lemon.

name: Casino #2
ingredients: ¾ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ shot Freshly squeezed orange juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Basically an Aviation but with a dash of orange juice and orange bitters.

name: Cassini
ingredients: ¼ shot Lejay Creme de Cassis de Dijon liqueur (18%)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Blackberry on rim or blackberries on stick
comment: A simple but pleasant spirituous berry drink. Not a Cassis Martini, but a Cassini.

name: Apairoteef
ingredients: 1 shot BarSol Supremo Mosto Verde Italia Pisco
instructions:
STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass (without ice). (Note the sparkling wine should be flat.)

*Special ingredient #1 = Cardamom & Pear Shrub made as follows:
400ml Merlet pare 
300ml Gewürztraminer, Germain. 
7g Cardamom pods
5ml Vanilla curd 
75ml White balsamic 
10g Citric acid 
10g Tartaric acid 
150g Caster sugar 
100g Water 
Blend for 5 minutes and super strain.
garnish: Set of confectionery dentures dropped into glass & lemon zest twist (discarded)
comment: Invigorating, refreshing and very amusing – we do like a pun.

name: Yuliya Martini
ingredients: ⅓ shot Aperitivo Cocchi Americano Bianco
2 dash La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Grapefruit zest twist & drop eucalyptus oil
comment: It’s surprising what influence just 2.5ml of Crème de Peche has on this subtly flavoured vodka-based cocktail, particularly if you omit the grapefruit twist.

name: Floral Daiquiri
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Le Sirop de Monin Lavandie - Lavender Sugar Syrup
instructions: Rinse chilled glass with violet liqueur and set aside ready for drink. MUDDLE blueberries in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into rinsed glass.
garnish: Sprig of dried lavender pegged to rim
comment: A floral riff on a classic Daiquiri with a hint of berry fruitiness and a lavender pink hue courtesy of a blueberry threesome.

name: Sleepy Smoker
ingredients: ⅓ shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled glass.
comment: Smoked vodka delicately fortifies dry faintly salty manzanilla sherry with curaçao adding a touch of sweetness and zesty orange freshness.

name: Eight Foot Fizz
ingredients: Top up with Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
garnish: Mint sprigs, dried citrus fruit wheels, cinnamon stick and red amaranth leaves, dusted with icing sugar. Place at least 5 pretzels over straw.

name: Instaspam
ingredients: 1 shot Boiron Cranberry & Morello cherry puree
garnish: Serve in a Spam tin and place tub of ice cream with small spoon on top of tin and rest straw across top.

name: Rabo-de-Galo
ingredients: ½ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
½ shot Cynar Ricetta Originale Artichoke Amaro Liqueur
instructions: THROW OR STIR (or even shake) all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: The bittersweet herbal notes from the vermouth and Cynar combine harmoniously with the cachaça. Depending on how punchy you like your cocktails you may want to increase or decrease the amount of cachaça (anywhere between 45 and 60ml).

name: Med Mex Cocktail
ingredients: 1 shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
1 shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
Top up with Three Cents Aegean Tonic Water
garnish: Long strip of cucumber peel around inside of glass.

name: Pink Americano
ingredients: Top up with Three Cents Pink Grapefruit Soda

name: Picasso Sour
ingredients: 1½ shot BarSol Supremo Mosto Verde Italia Pisco
½ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
⅚ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
4 dash La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Spray Angostura Bitters through stencil or drip a few drops on surface foam.
comment:
We were struck by the photograph that accompanied the recipe sent to us by this cocktail’s creator. The photo depicted the drink's presentation at Burj Al Arab in Dubai where it is served with a tiny wooden artist’s colour palate alongside micro easel holding a print of Picasso’s Portrait of The Absinthe Drinker.

The cocktail itself is an artistic citrus-led riff on a Pisco Sour, but the original presentation amounts to true cocktail artistry.

name: Champs-Elysees Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Chartreuse Verte (Green Chartreuse) Liqueur
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
½ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A great after dinner drink for lovers of cognac and Chartreuse.

name: Harvest Cocktail
ingredients: 1½ shot Cardamaro Amaro Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Boozy with notes of spiced apple brandy. A great after-dinner sipper.

name: Daisy de Santiago
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Oro Superior Gold Rum
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
¼ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
⅙ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
instructions: SHAKE first 4 ingredients with ice and strain into glass filled with crushed ice. DRIZZLE a spoon of Chartreuse over top of drink.
garnish: Seasonal berries & mint sprig or lime wheel (preferably dehydrated)
comment: Chartreuse adds subtle aromatics to this refreshing Daiquiri/Daisy.

name: Summer Americano
ingredients: 1 shot Martini Riserva Speciale Rubino Vermouth di Torino
⅙ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR first 4 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with a splash of tonic water.
comment: Aperitif-style, tall and refreshing.

name: Charles Daiquiri
ingredients: 1 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
½ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
⅛ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
½ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Navy rum and triple sec add special interest to this Daiquiri.

name: The Stag
ingredients: ⅓ shot Le Sirop de Monin Lavandie - Lavender Sugar Syrup
instructions: STIR all ingredients and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Orange zest twist (discarded) with cube of candied ginger
comment: Boozy and stirred down. Cognac fortifies and combines harmoniously with Jägermeister’s bittersweet herbal and spicy notes. Rich lavender enhances and amplifies these flavours with ginger bitters adding extra zingy spice and toning the blend. Best appreciated as an after-dinner digestive.

name: The Reindeer
ingredients: ⅔ shot Ketel One Citroen Vodka
¼ shot Le Sirop de Monin Spicy syrup
garnish: Holly sprig & cherry (the holly represents the antlers and the cherry the red nose)

name: Knickerbocker
ingredients: 2½ shot Bacardi Carta Oro Superior Gold Rum
⅙ shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE first 4 ingredients with ice and strain into glass 2/3rd filled with cubed ice. Cap with crushed ice, serve with a straw.
garnish: Squeezed-out half lime shell & seasonal berries: raspberries, blackberries, blueberries
comment: Fruity and refreshing – a party drink with rum and raspberry flavours predominating. At its best when made with homemade raspberry syrup.

name: A.B.C. Cocktail
ingredients: 1 shot Warre's Otima 10 Year Old Tawny Port
¼ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
⅛ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: TEAR mint and place in shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Lemon zest twist & Luxardo Maraschino cherry
comment: Wonderfully delicate. Mint gives subtle freshness to the classic port and brandy combo.

name: Dutch Artist's Special
ingredients: ½ shot G. Miclo Kirsch Vieux Grande Reserve Eau de Vie
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Bready genever is aromatised with sherry and kirsch while red currant syrup provides the main flavour with its richness balanced by lemon juice.

name: Slow Negroni
ingredients: ⅔ shot Rutte Sloe Gin liqueur
⅔ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
instructions: POUR ingredients into ice-filled glass and briefly STIR.
comment: As the name suggests, this is simply a classic Negroni with sloe gin introduced as a forth ingredient. Its flavour is exactly as it says on the tin – a sloe berry flavoured Negroni.

name: Gin Pahit
ingredients: ⅙ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: The balance of bitters to sugar is key to the success of this cocktail. Use a Japanese dasher bitters bottle and measure your 2:1 sugar syrup judiciously and you’ll have made something special. Go easy while spraying the lemon zest over the drink – too much lemon oil will wreck its fine balance.

name: Oaxacan Negroni
ingredients: ¾ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
¾ shot Cantine Gancia Antica Ricetta Barolo Chinato
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass (preferably over a chunk of block ice).
comment: Mezcal lends a characterful smoky note to this complex riff on a classic Negroni.

name: Suze you, Sir
ingredients: 2 shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: We do like a pun and appropriately the original recipe for this cocktail specifies Suze gentian liqueur. The flavour of gentian combines brilliantly with tequila while dry vermouth adds herbal complexity.

name: Maximiliano Cocktail
ingredients: 1¼ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
¾ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
¾ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
1/12 shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass (preferably over a chunk of block ice.
comment: The subtly smoky notes of mezcal combine brilliantly with the aromatic bitter sweetness of gentian liqueur in this fabulously complex aperitif cocktail.

name: Tara Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot John D. Taylor's Velvet Falernum Liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Imagine a delicately clove influenced Daiquiri but with the bready influence of poitín, then you are some way towards imagining the taste of this refreshing and invigorating cocktail.

name: Yellow Cactus Flower
ingredients: 1 shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
¾ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
¾ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Fresh pressed pineapple juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment:
Jordan Smith, describes his creation as “bright, herbaceous, subtly smoky, and refreshingly bittersweet. The pineapple adds texture and a slight sweetness to balance the tartness of the lime and bitterness of the Suze [Jordan’s gentain liqueur of choice], while the herbaceousness of the Suze is amplified by the Yellow Chartreuse.” We concur! “Bright, herbaceous, subtly smoky, and refreshingly bittersweet” perfectly sums up this challenging aperitif cocktail.

Jordan’s original spec calls for pineapple purée rather than juice. This adds mouthfeel and sweetness – consider muddling a couple of pineapple wedges in base of shaker with a dash of sugar syrup as an alternative.

name: Yellow Negroni
ingredients: ¾ shot BarSol Supremo Mosto Verde Italia Pisco
¾ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
¾ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
instructions: POUR all ingredients into serving glass, add ice to fill glass, STIR briefly and serve.
comment: As the name suggests, this pisco based, five equal part, vivid yellow bittersweet aperitif was inspired by the classic Negroni and three of the world’s most iconic yellow liqueurs: limoncello, gentian and Chartreuse Jaune.

name: Milanese G&T
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly STIR so as not to lose fizz.
comment: A traditional Gin & Tonic with a splash of Campari adding colour and bittersweet herbal fruity complexity.

name: Arte de Volar
ingredients: 1¾ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
½ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
¼ shot Créme de violette liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Tequila replaces gin in this riff on an Aviation – ‘Arte de Volar’ is Spanish for ‘Art of Flying. As with the original gin based classic, notes of maraschino and violet subtly influence with lemon juice freshening and adding balance.

name: Cadáver Reviver
ingredients: ¾ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
¾ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
¾ shot Lillet Blanc French aperitif wine
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
2 dash La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: The combination of tequila and triple sec make this riff on the classic Corpse Reviver remarkably Margarita-like, only with added complexity and subtlety due to the aromatised wine and faintest hint of absinthe.

name: Mexican Martini
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Tequila replaces vodka in this riff on the French Martini. Considering tequila is replacing vodka you’d think blanco tequila would work best, but after trying this and reposado we found añejo tequila works best with the tropical and berry fruits.

name: Dama Blanca
ingredients: 1½ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
¾ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅓ shot AquaRiva Organic Agave Syrup
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and strain into chilled glass.
comment: This tequila (in place of gin) based riff on a White Lady is basically a Margarita made with lemon rather than lime, softened and smoothed by egg white. This is a delightful drink. Go easy spraying the zest as this can overly dominate the drink. It also works best with a light agave syrup as the darker versions affect its virgin-like hue and can add unwanted caramel notes.

name: Negroni Bianco
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly STIR.
comment: This white Negroni is not quite as dry and bitter as a traditional red Negroni and has additional pleasing creamy honeysuckle notes.

name: Amber Negroni (D's recipe)
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly STIR.
comment: As the name suggests, this riff on a classic Negroni is amber in colour with honeyed notes and is not quite as bitter as the classic red version.

name: Tuxedo Affumicato
ingredients: 1⅓ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
⅔ shot Lillet Blanc French aperitif wine
⅓ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
1 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR first 4 ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Orange zest twist and a mist of Liquore Strega from an atomizer over drink
comment: Mezcal lends a lightly smoky note to this riff on the classic Tuxedo. This boozy Martini-esque cocktail makes for a great aperitif.

name: Pura Vida
ingredients: 1⅓ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Lightly smoked earthy coffee with hints of zesty orange.

name: Ginger Last Word
ingredients: ¾ shot Chartreuse Verte (Green Chartreuse) Liqueur
¾ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: MUDDLE ginger with gin in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: As the name suggests this is simply a Last Word with a hint of ginger. Simple can be beautiful.

name: Creme Egg Cocktail
ingredients: 1½ shot Ketel One Vodka (infused with vanilla)
½ shot Lightly spiced vanilla liqueur
½ shot Creme de cacao liqueur (white)
instructions:
1. To flavour your vodka with a vanilla pod. Simply cut the vanilla pod lengthways to expose the flavoursome seeds inside and drop the scored vanilla pod into your bottle of vodka. Reseal and shake bottle several times a day for at least two days, preferably a week.
2. SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Coat rim of glass with chocolate – use a microwave to melt a few squares of dark chocolate in a saucer and then and dip the rim of the glass into the melted chocolate. Grate chocolate over surface of the drink and add half a Cadbury Creme Egg skewered on a stick. It’s easier to cut the chocolate egg when straight from the refrigerator (a good test for how sharp your knife is). Spear the egg with a metal cocktail stick that has been heated under hot water.
comment: As befits the name, this chocolate and vanilla vodka-based Easter Egg of a cocktail has both cream and egg – the later in the form of Dutch advocaat.

name: Jäger Negroni
ingredients: ¾ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and briefly STIR. (Ideally, pre-mix ingredients and age in medium toasted oak barrel for one week.)
comment: A lightly spicy Negroni perfectly suited to the after-dinner “digestive” moment. If it doesn’t aid your digestion it will aid your sleep.

name: Duchess
ingredients: ½ shot La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Aniseed and liquorice notes from absinthe pervade this vermouth based cocktail, even though we have dramatically reduced the amount used from the original recipe which calls for equal parts of each ingredient. The Duchess is a drink for absinthe lovers.

name: Smoking Gun Vesper
ingredients: ¼ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Lemon zest twist & small strip cured smoked pork on a skewer.
comment: Delicately smoky and almost meaty – be sure to serve with cured meat. A great aperitif cocktail.

name: Celery Sour
ingredients: ⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅓ shot Fresh pressed pineapple juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: This aptly named drink is beautifully balanced with delicate celery flavours.

name: Celery Gimlet #2
ingredients: ¼ shot Chartreuse Verte (Green Chartreuse) Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
⅛ shot Chardonnay white wine vinegar
instructions: MUDDLE celery with salt in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Sweet ‘n’ sour, salt ‘n’ vinegar served with dry celery gin crunch – a cocktail to awaken your senses.

name: Espresso Martini (simple 3 equal parts recipe)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Balanced, tasty and enlivening, this cocktail is incredibly quick and easy to make using just three ingredients that should be every day essentials in every domestic kitchen.

name: Angels’ Advocate
ingredients: ⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Orange zest twist (cut to be cross-shaped)
comment: Advocaat, gin and lemon juice sit together harmoniously in this pale yellow cocktail with vanilla syrup adding balance and cardamom enlivening the blend.

name: Juan & Jeanne
ingredients: 1½ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
¾ shot Lejay Creme de Cassis de Dijon liqueur (18%)
½ shot Solerno Blood Orange Liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Tequila based with sour citrus balanced by the rich red wine-like berry flavours of cassis.

name: Black Mesa
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Ocho Anos Gran Reserva Rum
½ shot Smith & Cross Traditional Pot Still Jamaican Rum
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass (preferably over a chunk of block ice).
comment: Pungent, funky and boozy with harmonious rum dominated bitter sweetness.

name: Ill Viaggio
ingredients: 3 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass (preferably over a chunk of block ice).
comment: The ingredients might suggest that this cocktail should be stirred – we did try, but it benefits from the dilution and life that shaking imparts. After some elbow grease you’ll have a delicious bittersweet, herbal almond, after dinner drink.

name: Dutch Coupe
ingredients: 2 shot Rutte & ZN 12 Oude Graan Jenever
½ shot Carpano Antica Formula Vermouth
⅓ shot Cynar Ricetta Originale Artichoke Amaro Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredient with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Reminiscent of a genever manhattan served on the dry side with notes of Cynar.

name: Killer Cocktail
ingredients: ⅓ shot Giffard Passion Fruit (Fruit de la Passion) Syrup
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into glass filled with crushed ice.
garnish:
Lemon zest twist (cut like a shark fin) and 3 drops of Cranberry Bitters 
as a bloody shark attack (or use grenadine)
comment: Amaretto and passion fruit dominate this bitter-sweet sour.

name: The Artist's Muse
ingredients: 1 shot BarSol Supremo Mosto Verde Italia Pisco
1 shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
½ shot Crème de pêche (peach) liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Atomise absinthe over surface of cocktail
comment: Bittersweet and fruity, with complex aromatics from the pisco and strong flavours of gentian liqueur.

name: The Ribbon
ingredients: ⅓ shot Solerno Blood Orange Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Dry sherry and bitter orange with underlying ripe grape sweetness, The Ribbon makes for a perfect aperitif cocktail.

name: The Last Time (AKA Stop Fiddling With My Drink)
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Carta Oro Superior Gold Rum
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Square of white chocolate balanced on rim
comment: Richly flavoured and yet dry – rum and subtle desiccated coconut with monastic herbal sweetness dried by aromatic fino sherry and vanilla bitters.

name: She's Like
ingredients: ⅔ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: An unlikely combination of big rich flavours, but when served ice-cold this is either a very interesting Mediterranean aperitivo or a palate cleansing digestivo.

name: Via Roma
ingredients: ⅔ shot Cynar Ricetta Originale Artichoke Amaro Liqueur
⅔ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
⅔ shot Baliva Ferro China quinine liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: This aperitivo cocktail will appeal to lovers of Italian amari.

name: Pass the Dutchy
ingredients: ¾ shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
¼ shot Montenegro Amaro Premiata Specialita
¼ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
¼ shot Sauvignon blanc white wine
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: A dry, boozy sophisticated sipper of a cocktail with complex aromatic orange notes and underlying grape fruitiness. Works as both an aperitif or a digestive.

name: For Sake's Sake
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
⅓ shot Italicus Rosolio di Bergamotto Liqueur
⅓ shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Being dyslexic I’m bewildered as to how to spell dyslexic, while also amused by sake (the beverage brewed from rice) being spelt the same way as sake (as in for fuck’s sake!). Hopefully, you’ll find this aromatic, subtly orange influenced sake and rum-based cocktail both delightful to drink and easy to spell.

name: Old Hickory (Britten's recipe)
ingredients: 4 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Heavy doses of Peychaud's and orange bitters work with dry vermouth to balance this drink’s rich sweet vermouth base. A great aperitivo.

name: Blueberry Caipirinha
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)

name: Strawberry & Basil Caipirinha
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)

name: Bamboo Cocktail (Stuart's 1904 recipe)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Thought to be the original Bamboo recipe with 2 parts dry fino sherry to 1 part rich sweet vermouth (modern recipes are usually equal parts). Balanced and complex, heavier than Bamboo recipes with dry vermouth.

name: Kiwi Caipirinha
instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz/15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add slices of kiwi and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
comment: Fruity and refreshing, perfect for a summer’s afternoon.

name: Purple Stag
ingredients: ⅚ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Easy drinking and neither bitter or too sweet, it’s hard not to like this rich herbal fruity cocktail.

name: Bamboo Cocktail (Boothby's 1908 recipe)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Lemon zest twist (discarded) & olive - preferably pimiento-stuffed (‘pimola’ olive)
comment: This bone dry cocktail makes for a great aperitif, but you may find the Bamboo 'Perfect' version of this cocktail easier going.

name: Showgirl Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Aperol Aperitivo by Fratelli Barbieri
½ shot Martini Ambrato Riserva Speciale
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Wonderfully floral – flavours of rose and raspberry dominate with dry vermouth adding balance and complexity.

name: Embrasse de la Terre
ingredients: ¼ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: This dry spirituous aperitif, Martini-like, cocktail combines malty genever and bitter celery flavours with Yellow Chartreuse richness dried by vermouth.

name: Pomegranate Daiquiri
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Freshly pressed pomegranate juice is essential to the success of this deliciously fruity but dry daiquiri. Sadly, packaged juice doesn’t produce the same drink.

name: Scandinavian Daiquiri
ingredients: ¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with and strain into chilled glass.
comment: A caraway influenced riff on a daiquiri with aquavit replacing rum. Very refreshing and suited to a summer’s afternoon, or an early evening aperitif.

name: Courtside
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
Top up with Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
instructions: MUDDLE berries in base of shaker. Add next five ingredients (all but soda), SHAKE with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with soda.
garnish: Top with berries and mint sprig
comment: Easy drinking, fruity, refreshing and lightly sweet. Bring on the summer’s afternoon.

name: Watermelon Caipirinha
instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add watermelon and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
comment: The melon flavours are very subtle in this delicious Caipirinha.

name: Melon Caipirinha
instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add melon and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
comment: Exactly as the name suggests, a fruity, melon flavoured Caipirinha.

name: Melon & Apple Caipirinha
instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add melon and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. Add apple juice and STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
comment: Melon and lime dominate this deliciously fruity Caipirinha.

name: Pineapple Caipirinha
instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz / 15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add pineapple and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
comment: Natural acids from the pineapple make this more tart in flavour than some other fruit Caipirinhas, those with a sweet tooth may want to add an extra ½ spoon of sugar.

name: D's Fruit Cup No.3 (Cognac based)
ingredients: 1 shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
instructions: POUR all ingredients into glass. Half fill glass with ice. Add a citrus slice and a couple of mint leaves from garnish before filling to brim with ice. Finish with rest of garnish.
garnish: Lemon, orange & strawberry slices, mint sprig & borage
comment: Cognac works well in this fruit cup, rounding the other ingredients to present as a more harmonious blend when compared to the same recipe made with gin. However, it’s more of a winter’s cup, without the freshening edge expected of a summer’s fruit cup.

name: D's Fruit Cup No.6 (Vodka based)
ingredients: 1 shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
instructions: POUR all ingredients into glass. Half fill glass with ice. Add a citrus slice and a couple of mint leaves from garnish before filling to brim with ice. Finish with rest of garnish.
