1½ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
garnish: Lemon zest, cherry, cherry jam rim

name: Cherry-Let
ingredients: ⅔ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur

name: Heering is Keering
ingredients: 1⅓ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
1 shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
⅙ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)

name: Heering Whisper
ingredients: 2 shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur

name: Poision Lips
ingredients: ⅚ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
⅓ shot Grand Marnier Cordon Rouge Liqueur
½ gram Jalapeño or Fresno chilli pepper

name: Cherry Bomb
ingredients: ¾ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)

name: Copa Cola
ingredients: 1⅙ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
garnish: Vanilla bean, cut open to release aroma, dried lime wheel, cherry.

name: Peter and Charlie
ingredients: ⅔ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
½ shot De Kuyper Apricot Brandy Liqueur
⅚ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice

name: Code of Conduct
ingredients: ¾ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur

name: Patrón City Lights
ingredients: 1⅙ shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Giffard Passion Fruit (Fruit de la Passion) Syrup
2 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
Top up with Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
instructions:
POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and TOP with soda.

Nathan uses salted Peychaud's Bitters which he makes with Maldon salt (10g of Maldon salt to one bottle of Peychaud's Bitters).
comment: Created by Nathan Shearer of Callooh Callay, for Patrón Tequila at London Cocktail Week 2016.

name: Patrón Chipotle Punch
ingredients: 1⅓ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
⅚ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions:
SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.

Special Ingredient #1: Chipotle Oleo Scharum
comment: Created by José Luis León from Mexico. José is the general manager in Grupo Sicario and is the owner of Baltra and Licoreria Limantour and he created this cocktail for Patrón Tequila as part of their activity at London Cocktail Week 2016.

name: Patrón Señor Sling
ingredients: 1⅓ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
⅓ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
⅚ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
Top up with Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
instructions: SHAKE first 7 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with soda and lightly stir.
garnish: Dehydrated pineapple & lime slices with cherry
comment: Created by Philip Bischoff, bar manager at Singapore’s Four Seasons multi-award winning Hotel Bar, Manhattan. Philip created this drink for Patrón Tequila as part of their activity for London Cocktail Week 2016.

name: Patrónomy
ingredients: ⅚ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
⅚ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
2½ shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
instructions:
POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and STIR briefly.

Special Ingredient #1: Gentain eau-de-vie
garnish: Rim glass with black salt. Grapefruit zest twist
comment: Created by Remy Savage, the head bartender behind the celebrated Paris cocktail bar Little Red Door, for Patrón Tequila at London Cocktail Week 2016.

name: Mexica
ingredients: 1⅓ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions:
SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.

Special Ingredient #1: Homemade dill syrup
Add 160 grams of dill to 1 litre sugar syrup. Allow to infuse for at least 24 hours, strain and bottle.
comment: Created by Cihan Anadologlu, co-owner of Circle By Cihan Anadologlu in Munich, for Patrón Tequila at London Cocktail Week 2016.

name: Patrón Yellow Submarine
ingredients: 1⅓ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)

name: Mercado Roma
ingredients: 1⅔ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
1 shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
instructions:
SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass (preferably a chunk of block ice).

Special Ingredient #1: Pineapple Ancho Shrub
Blend equal parts pineapple juice and sugar and leave to rest for 2 hours. Add equal parts white wine vinegar and leave for 24 hours. Then add a teaspoon of chipotle sauce and blend.

Special Ingredient #2: Clove Honey
Add equal parts honey, water and a handful of cloves to a pot and heat at 180˚C for 25 minutes stirring constantly.
comment:
Created by Roberto Berdecia for Patrón Tequila at London Cocktail Week 2016.

Roberto is a co-founder of La Factoría, in San Juan, Puerto Rico.

name: Hotel Georgia Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: An almond gin sour with orange flower water adding a delicate floral note. Lighter and more pleasing than the recipe might suggest.

name: Celery Cocktail
ingredients: ¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Celery is usually only tasty when loaded with blue cheese. Vodka also seems to make it rather palatable.

name: Champagne Cocktail
instructions: COAT sugar cube with bitters and drop into glass. POUR chilled cognac over soaked cube, then TOP with champagne.
comment: Perhaps somewhat over-hyped, but this classic cocktail remains as popular as ever. Starts bone dry and becomes slightly sweeter as you reach the dissolving cube at the bottom, depending on how briskly you drink of course.

name: Champagne Cup
ingredients: ½ shot Grand Marnier Cordon Rouge Liqueur
¼ shot Maraschino syrup (from cherry jar)
instructions: STIR first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass. TOP with champagne and gently stir.
comment: Sweet maraschino helps balance this dry drink.

name: Prohibition Daisy (Non-alcoholic)
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into glass filled with crushed ice. CHURN drink and serve with straws.
comment: A lightly sweetened fruit smoothie with orange, pineapple and raspberry along with lime and lime which provide balancing citrus acidity.

name: 18th Century Cocktail
ingredients: 1½ shot By The Dutch Batavia Indonesian Arrack Buy from amazon
¾ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
¾ shot De Kuyper Apricot Brandy Liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Tart and dry with the unmistakable funky twang of Batavia arrack.

name: Cheeky Monkey
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Fire yellow in colour, this drink features the distinctive flavour of Chartreuse with a citrus supporting cast.

name: Chelsea Sidecar
ingredients: 1 shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Gin replaces cognac in this variation on the classic Sidecar.

name: Cherrute
ingredients: ½ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
1½ shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Grapefruit zest twist (discarded) & Luxardo Maraschino cherry
comment: Sweet cherry brandy balanced by the fruity acidity of grapefruit, laced with vodka.

name: Cherry Alexander
ingredients: ½ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
½ shot Creme de cacao liqueur (white)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A rich Black Forest Gateau like fruity twist on the creamy classic.

name: Nineteen Twenty Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot G. Miclo Kirsch Vieux Grande Reserve Eau de Vie
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Boozy and delicately flavoured – the combination of redcurrant syrup and kirsch is almost rose water-like.

name: Meh-Groni
ingredients: 2 shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
½ shot Punt E Mes vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: This Negroni-inspired cocktail will please mezcal loving bartenders and hardened drinkers (we also loved it) but may challenge other drinkers.

name: Original Sin (Gin Joint recipe)
ingredients: ½ shot Carpano Antica Formula Vermouth
⅓ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
⅔ shot Honey water (3 honey to 1 water)
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SWIZZLE all ingredients in a Julep cup with crushed ice.
comment: Lemon and honey with a touch of bitter-sweet flavoursome gentian liqueur.

name: Mexican Vineyard
ingredients: 1½ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: MUDDLE 8 grapes in base of shaker. ADD other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Perhaps best summarised as being an ‘Apple and Grape Margarita, the flavours combine harmoniously in what is a well-balanced drink.

name: Killer in Red
ingredients: ½ shot Grand Marnier Cordon Rouge Liqueur

name: Gringo's Blood & Sand
ingredients: ¾ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
¾ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
¾ shot Freshly squeezed orange juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Orange zest twist (discarded) & Luxardo maraschino cherry
comment: A Blood & Sand made with tequila. A classic Scotch whisky based Blood & Sand is made with equal parts of all four ingredients, but the cherry brandy, vermouth and orange seem to overwhelm tequila when used in place of Scotch, hence the much higher proportion of spirit in this riff.

name: Jalisco Mint Julep
ingredients: ⅔ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into julep cup half filled with crushed ice. CHURN (stir) the drink with the crushed ice using a bar spoon. Top up the cup with more crushed ice and CHURN again. Crown the drink with some fresh crushed ice, garnish and serve.
garnish: Mint sprigs dusted with icing sugar
comment: Given the use of tequila I initially opted for agave syrup over sugar syrup but I found the caramel notes that this added somewhat overwhelmed the mint. Instead, perhaps consider using demerara sugar in place of white cane syrup or a mix of the two. To be honest, I found myself preferring the original whiskey based Mint Julep when tasted against each other but I’d encourage you to give a tequila based Mint Julep a try, and if you have a better version then please email me at simon@Dsguide.com.

name: Tequila Club
ingredients: 1½ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
⅔ shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
⅓ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: The similarity of ingredients in this tequila based riff on the classic Pegu Club to the Margarita are obvious, so it’s not surprising the finished drink is reminiscent of a Margarita, only lighter with the lime notes a lot subtler. As well as the tequila, orange curacao shines in this drink.

name: Cherry Martini
ingredients: ¾ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
½ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A hint of cherry is balanced by citrus freshness, and dried and deepened by vermouth.

name: Stone Cold Fruit
ingredients: 1 shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
⅕ shot Carpano Antica Formula Vermouth
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Tart and dry with zesty orange and lemon. Mouth puckeringly refreshing.

name: Coffee Negroni
ingredients: ⅔ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly stir.
garnish: Orange zest twist & float 3 coffee beans
comment: Gin forward, boozy and bittersweet – as it says on the tin, this is a coffee flavoured Negroni – simple as that.

name: Avenue San Martin
ingredients: 1½ shot BarSol Supremo Mosto Verde Italia Pisco
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Bittersweet and tangy. A cocktail to enjoy as an aperitif with nibbles.

name: The Blind Side
ingredients: 1 shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
1 shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
garnish: Gently squeeze lemon wedge over drink and then drop into drink.
comment: Fresh and refreshing – a minty gentian aperitif.

name: Green Negroni
ingredients: ¾ shot Luxardo Bitter Bianco Liqueur
⅓ shot Chartreuse Verte (Green Chartreuse) Liqueur
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly STIR.
comment: Don’t be put off or misled by the lurid green colour or this cocktail, it is both bittersweet and complex. As the name suggests it is based in a classic Negroni but with Green Chartreuse herbal complexity and a dose of rich green melon liqueur.

name: Che's Revolution
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Complex and easy drinking with hints of maple syrup and mint amongst the pineapple and rum.

name: Dutchess
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
¾ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: One of those Angostura heavy (very heavy) cocktails intuition says just shouldn’t work (the name’s misspelt for starters,) but thanks to the rich balancing properties of almond syrup and pineapple it is not only balanced but good. The genever base justifies the name and adds its distinctive character.

name: Eggs-cellent
ingredients: ½ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Brandy and advocaat (the eggy element) is a classic and indulgent combo that says Easter and/or Christmas – both spiritual holidays so the addition of Chartreuse is obvious. As this Eggs-cellent cocktail was made for Easter it had to have some chocolate so we added chocolate bitters.

name: Grand Duchess Cocktail
ingredients: ⅓ shot Smith & Cross Traditional Pot Still Jamaican Rum
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
½ shot Giffard Sirop Grenadine (Grenadine Syrup)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: This vodka-based Tiki-style cocktail is made “funky” by a splash of pot still Jamaican rum while lime juice adds freshness and grenadine adds both a rich hue and sweetness.

name: Rum & IT
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
1½ shot Italicus Rosolio di Bergamotto Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Created in April 2017 by yours truly (Simon D) at the Cabinet Room, London, England.

name: Chocolate Rum & Raisin
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Ocho Anos Gran Reserva Rum
½ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
¼ shot Gabriel Boudier Dark Creme de Cacao Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Squares raisin & nut chocolate balanced across rim
comment: This cocktail delivers pretty much what it says on the tin – chocolate, rum and raisin. Best enjoyed after dinner with deluxe similarly flavoured chocolate.

name: Ginger & Fig Caipirinha
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)

name: Cucumber Jalapeño Caipirinha
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)

name: Banana Caipirinha
ingredients: ½ shot Giffard Banane du Brésil crème de banane liqueur
instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add 3 bar spoons caster sugar (or ½oz/15ml sugar syrup 2:1) and MUDDLE.

3. Add slices of banana and MUDDLE some more.

4. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
comment: With an overripe banana this drink is delicious, thick and creamy. A meal in itself.

name: Tessmanian Devil
ingredients: ¾ shot Liquore Strega Giuseppe Alberti
¾ shot Lillet Blanc French aperitif wine
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Two fresh red chilli peppers positioned as devil's horns on rim
comment: The “devil” is in this cocktail’s hot spice. Obviously the garnish should not be eaten.

name: Naughty German
ingredients: ⅓ shot Lejay Creme de Cassis de Dijon liqueur (18%)
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Jägermeister spice and rich cassis combine wonderfully in this after dinner sipper, with subtle black liquorice and aniseed.

name: Chimayo
ingredients: 2 shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
½ shot Lejay Creme de Cassis de Dijon liqueur (18%)
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Apple juice and cassis take the sting off tequila.

name: Black Forest Sour
ingredients: ⅙ shot Cherry Heering cherry brandy liqueur
⅓ shot Fresh pressed pineapple juice
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and pour into ice-filled glass.
comment: Beautifully balanced with complex herbal, berry, pineapple and citrus flavours.

name: China Beach
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Dry and lightly spiced.

name: China Blue Cocktail
ingredients: 1 shot Lejay Curacao Bleu (blue curacao) Liqueur
2 shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: This simple cocktail with its turquoise colour tastes more adult and interesting than its colour might suggest.

name: Blackberry Agave Caipirinha
instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and then slice one of the halves into eight 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass and MUDDLE.

2. Add blackberries and MUDDLE some more.

3. When sure fruit juice is fully extracted and the sugar dissolved, transfer the muddled fruit and sugar into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

4. Add agave syrup to serving glass.

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to ensure all ingredients are well mixed.

6. Crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
comment: Sweetening with agave nectar rather than sugar helps set this fruity Caipirinha apart and creates a drink that is very slightly sweet.

name: The Joanna
ingredients: ¾ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Small lavender sprigs pegged to rim
comment: Reminiscent of a vodkatini with fino sherry and notes of floral elderflower and lavender.

name: Dutch Sour
ingredients: ½ shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: A floral orangey riff on a vodka sour.

name: French Kiwi & Apple Caipirinha
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)

name: Chinese Cosmopolitan
ingredients: ¾ shot Kwai Feh lychee liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Oriental in name and style - perhaps a tad sweeter than your standard Cosmo.

name: D's Fruit Cup No.11 (Old Tom gin based)
ingredients: 1 shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
instructions: POUR all ingredients into glass. Half fill glass with ice. Add a citrus slice and a couple of mint leaves from garnish before filling to brim with ice. Finish with rest of garnish.
garnish: Lemon, orange & strawberry slices, mint sprig & borage
comment: It’s is possible, indeed probable, that the originial Pimm’s Cup served in the eponymous London bar was in fact based on old tom gin rather than London dry gin. However, while flavoursome, old tom does inherently produce a sweeter style drink compared to when the same recipe is made with dry gin or other base spirits.

name: Gin & IT (Rosolio recipe)
ingredients: 1½ shot Italicus Rosolio di Bergamotto Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Bitter orange peel is a botanical which features in many gins, and in this cocktail a bitter sweet bergamot liqueur is mixed with gin to good effect.

name: IPAlicus
ingredients: 1 shot Italicus Rosolio di Bergamotto Liqueur
instructions: POUR ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly stir.
comment: Take a citrusy hoped beer and add a tad more citrus in the shape of Rosolio di Bergamotto. The result is predictably citrusy, refreshing and suited to the aperitivo moment rather than a night down the Rose & Crown.

name: Caribbean Colada
ingredients: 1 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
1 shot Bacardi Ocho Anos Gran Reserva Rum
1 shot Giffard Premium Caribbean Pineapple Liqueur Buy from amazon
⅙ shot Giffard Passion Fruit (Fruit de la Passion) Syrup
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into glass filled with crushed ice.
comment: Rum and pineapple strike up a tropical harmony, helped by coconut, passionfruit and orange.

name: Tropical Pineapple Daiquiri
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
1 shot Giffard Premium Caribbean Pineapple Liqueur Buy from amazon
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
⅓ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into an ice-filled glass.
comment: Pineapple and rich almond flavours add a tropical touch to this refreshing Daiquiri.

name: Chinese Whisper
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: MUDDLE ginger in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: There's more than a whisper of ginger in this spicy cocktail.

name: Soixante-Quinze (Washington Herald 1915 recipe)
ingredients: ⅔ shot Laird's Straight Bottled in Bond applejack
⅓ shot Giffard Sirop Grenadine (Grenadine Syrup)
⅙ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: This 1915 recipe, originally of equal parts gin, applejack and grenadine (1/3 of each to 1/6 lemon juice) makes this Seventy-Five a tad on the sweet side (certainly sweet if you are using a proprietary or housemade 2:1 grenadine). However, who knows what brix the original grenadine/pomegranate syrup was, so we’ve eased back the grenadine to bring modern day ingredients more into kilter. The resulting drink (which benefits from a splash of water to aid dilution), remains more a fruity spirituous after-dinner cocktail rather than an aperitif.

name: Limoncello Spritz
ingredients: ⅔ shot Soda (club soda) or sparkling water

name: PLB
instructions: MUDDLE lemon in base of a sturdy rocks glass and then pour into serving glass. Fill glass with ice, add limoncello and bitters. TOP with tonic and lightly stir.
comment: Long and refreshing with a tart lemony bite.

name: Spiked Arnold Palmer
ingredients: 4 shot Freshly brewed cold black English breakfast tea
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and stir.
comment: Tea tannins and sour lemon balance rich limoncello in this refreshing low alcohol cocktail. Perfect for summer.

name: Black Basil
ingredients: ½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: MUDDLE basil in base of shaker. Add next 4 ingredients (all but tonic water), SHAKE with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with tonic water and lightly stir.
comment: Light in alcohol, with its basil and zesty lemon flavours, this wholesome looking cocktail makes for a refreshing summer afternoon drink.

name: Nobile Cocktail
instructions: MUDDLE raspberries in base of shaker. Add next 4 ingredients (all but bitter lemon), SHAKE with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with bitter lemon and lightly stir.
garnish: Lemon slice, mint sprig, raspberry & blackberry
comment: Fruity but not sweet, with drying bitter lemon. This is a refreshing, low alcohol, aperitif style summery cocktail.

name: Chocolate & Cranberry Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Creme de cacao liqueur (white)
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled, rimmed glass.
comment: The chocolate rim sounds naff but makes this drink. Surprisingly dry.

name: Biblical Quartet
instructions: SHAKE first three ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass. TOP with dry cider.
garnish: Apple slice or dehydrated apple slice
comment: Four different apple products – three of them alcoholic – combined harmoniously in one cocktail, so obviously appley and just on the dry side of balanced.

name: Flat White Martini
ingredients: 1½ shot Baileys Original Irish Cream liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Martini drinkers of the gin/vodka and vermouth variety will describe this drink as being all too flat and all too white, but for lovers of flat white coffees and cream liqueurs, this Martini will hit the spot – even though it’s not really a Martini.

name: Merry Widow Fizz
ingredients: 1 spoon Powdered sugar (or use 5ml of 2:1 sugar syrup per spoon)
3 shot Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
instructions: SHAKE first 5 ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and strain into chilled glass (no ice). TOP with soda.
comment: Rich berry balanced by citrus and charged with soda to fizz. The egg white ensures a lasting thick foaming head.

name: Sex On The Beach (D's recipe)
ingredients: ⅔ shot Original Peachtree Liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients (the ‘beach’ part) with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. Then SHAKE the last 3 ingredients (the ‘sex’ part) with ice and strain into glass to sit on top of the ‘beach’ base layer. Serve with stirrer and instruct drinker to mix layers together before drinking.
comment:
Sweet fruitiness masks the alcohol in this easy drinking, long cocktail.

“Sex” is associated with red – think red light district, and is represented in this drink in the form of vodka, cranberry juice, and black raspberry liqueur. These sexy ingredients sit atop the sandy yellow "beach"– consisting peach schnapps, orange juice, and pineapple juice.

name: Sloe Gin Cocktail
ingredients: ¼ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Sloe gin comprises such a large part of this cocktail that the style and quality of your sloe gin will dramatically affect the finished cocktail. Choose well and this is a delicious, well-balanced, wintery cocktail.

name: Slow Comfortable Screw Against a Cold Hard Wall With a Kiss
ingredients: ½ shot Rutte Sloe Gin liqueur
½ shot Disaronno Originale Amaretto liqueur
instructions: STIR first 4 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. Then FLOAT amaretto so it bleeds into drink. Finish with a FLOAT of Galliano and overproof rum.
comment: The float of amaretto adds the “Kiss” to a Slow Comfortable Screw Against a Cold Hard Wall. If this was served in a Tiki mug it would be a Tiki Drink rather than a Disco Drink – it’s fruity and boozy, and just edging towards the sweet side with that splash of amaretto adding rich almond notes.

name: Chocolate Martini
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Vodka and chocolate made more interesting with a hint of vermouth.

name: Slow Comfortable Fuzzy Screw Against the Wall
ingredients: ½ shot Rutte Sloe Gin liqueur
½ shot Original Peachtree Liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR first 4 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. FLOAT sloe gin on surface so it bleeds into drink. Finish with Galliano FLOAT.
comment: Peach schnapps is borrowed from the Fuzzy Navel to influence this member of the Slow Comfortable family, its presence gives this cocktail a distinctive sweet peachy note.

name: Alabama Slammer (long)
ingredients: ½ shot Disaronno Originale Amaretto liqueur
½ shot Rutte Sloe Gin liqueur
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. (Don’t be tempted to shake as this detrimentally thins the drink. Instead, consider rolling or throwing.)
comment: A Screwdriver that's flavoured and lightly sweetened with amaretto, Southern Comfort and sloe gin.

name: Hairy Navel
ingredients: 1 shot Original Peachtree Liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. (Don’t be tempted to shake as this detrimentally thins the drink. Instead, consider rolling or throwing.)
comment: A Screwdriver with added peach schnapps liqueur. It’s fruity and on the sweet side of balanced – much tastier than a Fuzzy Navel.

name: Koko Colada
ingredients: 1¼ shot Bacardi Carta Oro Superior Gold Rum
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A ridiculously simple party drink with three equal parts shaken to produce a pineapple and coconut cocktail fortified with rum and crowned with a foamy head. Off dry and fruity rather than sweet.

name: Chocolate Mint Martini
ingredients: ½ shot Giffard Menthe Pastille white crème de menthe liqueur
½ shot Creme de cacao liqueur (white)
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: An after dinner sweety that tastes of chocolate mints.

name: Vodka Soda
instructions: POUR ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly stir (so as not to lose fizz). Don’t serve with straws.
comment:
Dry and refreshing with a pleasant minerality.

The proportion of soda water to vodka in this recipe is 2.5 to 1 (a ratio that appears to have the widest appeal) consisting of one 150 ml can of soda water to 60 ml vodka = 210ml plus ice melt total liquid in glass. If using a 12oz glass (350ml to fill level) the ice displacement = approximately 140ml, so ice forms 40% of contents of glass. To raise the percentage of ice to the ideal of around 50% you should use a 14oz Collins glass or a Spanish style Copa glass.

name: Chocolate Puff
ingredients: 1 shot Bacardi Carta Oro Superior Gold Rum
1 shot Gabriel Boudier Dark Creme de Cacao Liqueur
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Smooth as you like. The orange is surprisingly evident.

name: Rum & Raisin Espresso Martini
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Ocho Anos Gran Reserva Rum
¾ shot Espresso coffee (freshly made & hot)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A hint of PX sherry adds the rainsy notes to this rum based riff on Dick Bradsell’s Espresso Martini.

name: Mexican Squeeze
ingredients: 1⅔ shot Patrón Silver Tequila (green seal) 100% agave tequila
⅓ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly stir.
comment: The success of this cocktail is as much about the quality of your tequila as it is about your choice of lemonade. Made well and this Squeeze is zesty, balanced and very refreshing.

name: Apicius
ingredients: ⅓ shot Edmond Briottet Crème de Châtaigne Liqueur
⅓ shot Pierre Ferrand Ancienne Methode Dry Curacao
⅙ shot Fernet Branca digestive bitter liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: This digestive cocktail is aromatic and bittersweet with herbal and botanical complexity rounded by rich chestnut liqueur.

name: Pterodactyl Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
1/12 shot La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: THROW all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Adapted from a drink created in 2017 by Martyn Lourencoat at Kol Restaurant, Reykjavik, Iceland, who says, "Since cocktail culture is still pretty new in Iceland and people aren't used to gentiane and absinthe in their drinks, I chose the name Pterodactyl because I didn't want guests to connect the name to anything they've had before! The fact that it is difficult to pronounce is an advantage as it is a good talking point for us. Pterodactyl is the name of a species of Pterosaur and so is very hard to come up with expectations on taste from the name!

name: Chocolate Sidecar
ingredients: 1 shot Gabriel Boudier Dark Creme de Cacao Liqueur
1 shot Warre's Otima 10 Year Old Tawny Port
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.

name: Flor De Don Juan
ingredients: 1⅔ shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: MUDDLE basil in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: A basil tequila sour (Margarita if you will) sweetened with elderflower liqueur – basil and elderflower flavours sit well with the tequila and lime.

name: Cider Apple Cooler
ingredients: 1 shot Berentzen Apfel Korn (apple schnapps) liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Not unlike the taste of strong dry cider.

name: Cider Apple Cocktail
ingredients: ¾ shot Berentzen Apfel Korn (apple schnapps) liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice & fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: As the name suggests, rich cider flavours with a sharp finish.

name: Washington's Applejack Mule
ingredients: 1½ shot Laird's Straight Bottled in Bond applejack
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with ginger beer and lightly stir.
comment: Take a Moscow Mule and make with apple brandy in place of vodka and you’ve a Washington's Applejack Mule. Tasty – arguably tastier than a Moscow Mule.

name: Don's Coco Punch
ingredients: 1⅔ shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila
⅓ shot Chartreuse Jaune (Yellow Chartreuse) Liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Reminiscent of a tequila Piña Colada with the merest hint of Chartreuse.

name: Susto
ingredients: 1 shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
⅚ shot Montenegro Amaro Premiata Specialita
½ shot Byrrh Grand Quinquina Aromatized Wine
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass (preferably over a chunk of block ice).
comment: Bittersweet with mezcal and amaro combining harmoniously along with aromatic wine and celery bitters.

name: G+T Royale
instructions: POUR all ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly stir.
comment: A summery aperitif gin cocktail with crisp tonic water and enlivening champagne, flavoured with hints of vanilla and orange.

name: The Alchemist
ingredients: 1⅙ shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
⅔ shot Luxardo Maraschino Originale Liqueur
½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
⅔ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Looking over the liqueur and syrup dominated recipe this mezcal based dry would appear to be sweet. However, the spec is deceiving, and the finished drink is surprisingly balanced, harnessing some big flavours and presenting them harmoniously.

name: Le Very French 75
ingredients: 1 shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE first 4 ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass. TOP with champagne.
garnish: Lemon zest twist & two amarena cherries
comment: A dry, bitter aperitif-style riff on the classic French 75. Try with a splash more champagne than suggested in the recipe and/or a spoon of sugar syrup.

name: Triangle
ingredients: ⅔ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
⅔ shot Poire William eau de vie
⅔ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: A bite sized bittersweet aperitif style cocktail.

name: Paycheck
ingredients: 3 dash La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
2 shot Rutte Dry Gin (infused with Earl Grey tea)
½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
½ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
⅓ shot Honey water (3 honey to 1 water)
instructions: RINSE chilled glass with absinthe and set aside. STIR other ingredients with ice and strain into absinthe rinsed glass.
comment: Spirituous and aromatic with rich honey notes balancing bitter gentian.

name: And Now for Something Suze
ingredients: ½ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Lemon zest twist (discarded) & fresh thyme sprig on floating dehydrated lemon wheel
comment: Gary’s original recipe for this dry and spirituous aperitif cocktail calls for Navy strength gin but we found it punchy enough with a gin at 40 – 43% alc./vol..

name: Citrus Caipirovska
ingredients: ¾ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: MUDDLE lemon in base of glass. Add other ingredients and fill glass with crushed ice. CHURN drink with bar spoon and serve with short straws.
comment: Superbly refreshing balance of sweet and citrus sourness.

name: Citrus Martini
ingredients: ¼ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice & fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Orange undertones add citrus depth to the lemon explosion.

name: Mandarine Daisy
ingredients: ¾ shot Mandarine Napoleon Grande Liqueur Imperiale
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
⅓ shot AquaRiva Organic Agave Syrup
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: “Citrusy and sour” sums up this refreshing gin laced cocktail with rich mandarin orange liqueur soured by lime.

name: Claret Cobbler
ingredients: 1 shot Grand Marnier Cordon Rouge Liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice & fine strain into glass filled with crushed ice. Serve with straws.
comment: Fortified and slightly sweetened wine cooled and lengthened by ice.

name: Am I Blue?
ingredients: 1 shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE first 4 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with pale ale and briefly stir.
comment: Long and refreshing, based on India pale ale and birch spirit with grapefruit freshness.

name: Bermuda Triangle
ingredients: 1 shot By The Dutch Batavia Indonesian Arrack Buy from amazon
¾ shot Fresh pressed pineapple juice
1 spoon Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
1 dash Dale DeGroff's Pimento Aromatic Bitters
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into a chilled glass.
comment: As Ralf, the drinks creator, says, "The Batavia plays well with the pineapple and the gomme, and the sherry gives it depth and body” to produce a punch-style cocktail.

name: GDPR Cocktail
ingredients: 2 shot BarSol Supremo Mosto Verde Italia Pisco
1 shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
½ shot Demerara sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)

name: Clover Leaf Cocktail No.1 (Classic Formula)
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
¼ shot Giffard Sirop Grenadine (Grenadine Syrup)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice & fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Smooth, aromatic, fruity and medium sweet.

name: Club Cocktail (Butt's recipe)
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Oro Superior Gold Rum
½ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
½ shot Maraschino syrup (from cherry jar)
¾ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice & fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: An aromatic, spirited, classical cocktail.

name: Cobbled Raspberry Cocktail
ingredients: ¼ shot Lejay Creme de Framboise (raspberry) Liqueur
⅓ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A simple 1990s Raspberry 'Martini' with a splash of wine.

name: Coconut Daiquiri
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
¾ scoop Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice & fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Classic Daiquiri flavour with a pleasing tropical touch.

name: Coconut Water
ingredients: 1¼ shot Chilled water (reduce if wet ice)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Have you ever drunk from a fresh coconut in the Caribbean? Well, this is London's alcoholic equivalent.

name: Cold Comfort
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Take at the first sign of a cold, and then retreat under your bed covers. Repeat dose regularly while symptoms persist. Warning - do not consume with other forms of medication.

name: Armenita Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Liquore Strega Giuseppe Alberti
½ shot De Kuyper Apricot Brandy Liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into an ice-filled glass.
comment: Reminiscent of an apricot and herbal Margarita. Consider adding a pinch of salt and a dash of apricot bitters.

name: The Frank O'Hara
ingredients: ⅓ shot Liquore Strega Giuseppe Alberti
instructions: STIR with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: This ever so simple Martini-style cocktail is surprisingly complex and tasty with the botanicals of both the gin and Italian liqueur combining harmoniously.

name: Golden Tang Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Giffard Banane du Brésil crème de banane liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed orange juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: This vodka laced cocktail is indeed tangy and golden (well light orange) with a pleasing banana chew flavour.

name: Angela Luce
ingredients: ⅓ shot Giffard Sirop Grenadine (Grenadine Syrup)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Fruity and herbal – a refreshingly zingy nightcap.

name: Lightness Cocktail
ingredients: ¾ shot Ketel One Citroen Vodka
¾ shot Liquore Strega Giuseppe Alberti
¾ shot Giffard Banane du Brésil crème de banane liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
garnish: Lemon zest twist & Luxardo Maraschino cherry
comment: A citrus vodka sour with Strega and crème de banana.

name: Witches' Smile
ingredients: ½ shot Liquore Strega Giuseppe Alberti
¾ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: This tall vodka laced drink is lightly herbal, fruity and refreshing.

name: Witches' Yellow Flip
ingredients: ¾ shot Freshly squeezed orange juice
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Strega liqueur and citrus juice fortified with cognac flavour this aptly named flip.

name: Lazy Lover
ingredients: 1 shot Del Maguey VIDA de San Luis del Rio 100% agave blanco mezcal Buy from amazon
¾ shot Suze gentian liqueur Buy from amazon
¼ shot Demerara sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
¼ shot Freshly squeezed pink grapefruit juice
instructions: MUDDLE cucumber slices and salt in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Savory and lightly bittersweet. An interesting aperitif cocktail.

name: Tuxedo Cocktail No.1 (Savoy & Duffy's recipe)
ingredients: 2 dash La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: Express lemon zest twist into stirring glass. Add other ingredients, STIR with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Bone dry with faint anise and liquorice notes from the absinthe.

name: Tuxedo Cocktail No.3 (Lowe & Straub's recipe)
ingredients: ⅔ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
1 dash La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Although only a spoonful, the sherry used dramatically impacts the finished cocktail. We tried amontillado but found this drink works best with either fino sherry or manzanilla sherries.

name: Tuxedo Cocktail No.4 (Old Waldorf Astoria recipe)
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Bone dry and aromatic with green grape, citrus and mineral notes. Also consider garnishing this cocktail with an olive – it suits it. This is a tasty riff on a Dry Martini but it has no vermouth (it uses sherry instead) so isn’t really a Dry Martini or even a Tuxedo.

name: Hollywood Blvd
ingredients: 1⅔ shot Bacardi Ocho Anos Gran Reserva Rum
⅚ shot Fresh pressed pineapple juice
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Mustard is perhaps an unlikely fit with rum, pineapple and lemon juice but they sit together in this very unusual daiquiri-style cocktail. Be careful not to overdo the mustard.

name: Zima Sour
ingredients: ⅔ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ shot Giffard Passion Fruit (Fruit de la Passion) Syrup
⅙ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
3 dash La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Aromatic and perfumed, bison grass, apple, sour lemon and rich passion fruit combine in this lightly sweet and fruity cocktail.

name: Dark 'n' Stormy Highball
instructions:
Integrated version: POUR ingredients into ice-filled glass and briefly stir. 

Layered version: POUR ginger beer into ice-filled glass first, then float rum.
garnish: Squeeze a wedge of lime into drink and drop in as a garnish
comment: Refreshing on a hot summers day and darkly appropriate for Halloween.

name: Carriaggi Cocktail
ingredients: 1 shot G. Miclo Kirsch Vieux Grande Reserve Eau de Vie
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: The holy trinity - three equal parts: kirschwasser, Strega liqueur and fresh orange juice. The result is surprisingly spirit-forward, fruity and refreshing.

name: Vive l'apéritif
ingredients: 1⅔ shot Lillet Blanc French aperitif wine
instructions: POUR ingredients into ice-filled glass.
garnish: Cucumber slice, and preferably also strawberry, & mint
comment: Thirst quenching, refreshing and low in alcohol. A great drink for a summer's afternoon or even before lunch.

name: Black Rum Swizzle
ingredients: ¼ shot John D. Taylor's Velvet Falernum Liqueur
instructions: POUR all ingredients into a glass half filled with crushed ice. SWIZZLE and then fill the glass with more crushed ice.
comment: Dark rum adds depth of flavour to this fruity and refreshing cocktail.

name: Black Rumhattan
ingredients: ⅚ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth

name: Caipirinha (no sugar & low-calorie)
instructions:
1. Cut lime in half (from pole to pole) and slice on half into 3-4mm thick half-moon shaped segments and drop into the base of a robust rocks glass.

2. Add sweetener and MUDDLE.

3. If using cubed ice straight from the freezer, add chilled water (or optionally add fruit of your choice) and MUDDLE some more. If using watery ice then omit water.

4. When sure muddling has fully extracted fruit juice, POUR the muddled fruit and sweetener into serving glass (preferably a 30cl / 10oz hi-ball glass).

5. POUR cachaça (stored in the freezer) into the rocks glass (the glass used to muddle) and swirl to rinse glass before pouring cachaça into serving glass. STIR to mix with other ingredients.

6. If using cubed ice, crack (rather than crush) four to five large ice cubes and drop into serving glass. STIR before topping with more ice and serve with a stirrer (but no straws).
garnish: Stirrer (spoon, wooden lolly stick or stick cut from sugar cane)
comment: Forget those Caipirinhas blighted by crunchy undissolved sugar crystals, this version is sugar-free.

name: Cosmopolitan (no sugar & low-calorie)
ingredients: ½ shot Giffard Agave Sec liqueur
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass. (Be sure to use naturally sweetened cranberry drink rather than “juice” sweetened with sugar.)
comment: The cranberry juice you select and even some vodkas may introduce unwanted sugar into this cocktail, so choose judiciously. We’ve used Agave Sec as our orange liqueur due to its being exclusively sweetened with agave nectar.

name: Cool Orchard
ingredients: 1½ shot Bacardi Ocho Anos Gran Reserva Rum
¼ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: MUDDLE ginger in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: An unusual line up of ingredients combine to make a great drink.

name: Passion Fruit Altern’atini (no sugar & low-calorie)
instructions: Cut passion fruits in half and scoop out flesh into shaker (keep one half for garnish). Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A couple of dashes orange bitters helps add a touch of complexity and balance to this vodka-laced fruity cocktail, but beware those bitters may add trace amounts of sugar.

name: Vodka Collins (no sugar & low-calorie)
ingredients: 3 shot Soda (club soda) or sparkling water
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with soda and briefly stir.
comment: Cleansing, easy drinking, sugar-free and just 100 calories.

name: White Lady (no sugar & low-calorie)
ingredients: ½ shot Giffard Agave Sec liqueur
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain back into shaker. DRY SHAKE (without ice) and fine strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Lemon and orange meringue with light gin botanical notes.

name: Copper Illusion
ingredients: ¾ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: Basically a Negroni with liqueur replacing sweet vermouth. Like the Italian classic this is both bitter and sweet.

name: Cosmopolitan Cocktail (Dale DeGroff's recipe)
ingredients: ½ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: This is the definitive Cosmo recipe from the man most associated with the drinks development and popularity.

name: Cosmopolitan Delight
ingredients: ½ shot Grand Marnier Cordon Rouge Liqueur
¼ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
¾ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: No relation to the modern Cosmopolitan, this is a mellow, balanced blend of citrus, brandy and red wine.

name: Creamy Bee
ingredients: ½ shot Baileys Original Irish Cream liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass
comment: Creamy cinnamon with hints of honey, nuts and berries.

name: Crème Anglaise Cocktail
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Very reminiscent of alcoholic crème anglaise.

name: Cuba Libre
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Oro Superior Gold Rum
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: POUR ingredients into ice-filled glass and lightly stir.
comment: Basically a rum and coke with a squeeze of lime, but Cuba Libre has much more of a ring about it. And it is much more of a drink, the squeeze of lime and dashes of bitters add layers of complexity, balancing the sweetness of the cola.

name: Cubanita
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass.
comment: The Bloody Mary returns - this time with rum.

name: Almond Martini #1
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ shot Giffard Sirop de Orgeat (Almond) Syrup
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Almond inspired with hints of apple and lemon juice.

name: Cucumber & Mint Martini
ingredients: ¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: MUDDLE cucumber and mint in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A well balanced fortified salad in a glass - almost healthy.

name: Cucumber Sake-Tini
ingredients: ¼ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: MUDDLE cucumber in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Subtle and dry. Cucumber and sake are made for each other.

name: Cumbersome
ingredients: ½ shot Sugar syrup (2 sugar to 1 water)
instructions: MUDDLE cucumber in base of shaker. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and strain into a chilled Martini glass.
comment: Interesting and fresh as you like with a pleasant bitterness.

name: Cuppa Joe
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Nutty coffee fortified with vodka - well balanced.

name: Curdish Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: STIR lemon curd with gin in base of shaker until curd dissolves. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: Beautifully balanced with the tang of lemon curd.

name: Custard Tart
ingredients: ¾ shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
½ shot Original Peachtree Liqueur Buy from amazon
¼ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
instructions: MUDDLE physalis fruits in base of shaker can. Add other ingredients, SHAKE with ice and strain.
comment: Custardy, strangely enough.

name: Absinthe Drip Cocktail #1 (French method)
ingredients: 1½ shot La Fée Parisienne Absinthe Superieure
instructions: POUR absinthe into glass. PLACE cube of sugar on a slotted absinthe spoon resting across the top of the glass. Using an absinthe fountain (or a bottle of chilled mineral water with a small hole in the cap), DRIP iced water over the sugar so it dissolves and drips into the glass. Traditionally the same amount of water is added as absinthe, but full-strength absinthe requires more dilution - Keep dripping until absinthe louches (turns milky white). Add ice, stir and serve.
comment: Patience is a virtue. Slow dripping of the water is essential to dissolve the entire sugar cube and give the drink enough sweetness to balance the absinthe.

name: Almond Martini #2
ingredients: ¾ shot Disaronno Originale Amaretto liqueur
¼ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
¾ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.
comment: A delicate, almond flavoured Vodka Martini.

name: Adelaide Swizzle
ingredients: 2 shot Bacardi Carta Blanca Superior White Rum (37.5%)
¾ shot John D. Taylor's Velvet Falernum Liqueur
½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice
2 dash Peychaud's Aromatic Bitters Buy from amazon
instructions: POUR ingredients into chilled glass and half fill with crushed ice. SWIZZLE with a swizzle stick (or churn with a barspoon). Add more crushed ice to fill and SWIZZLE some more. Serve with straws.
comment: A slightly pink, dry, spicy long drink with rum and a hint of cloves and lime.

name: Alfonso Martini
ingredients: 1 shot Grand Marnier Cordon Rouge Liqueur
½ shot Martini Extra Dry vermouth
¼ shot Martini Rosso sweet vermouth
½ shot Chilled water (omit if using wet ice)
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into a chilled glass.
comment: Dry yet slightly sweet with hints of orange, gin and warm spice.

name: Amsterdam Cocktail
ingredients: 1 shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
instructions: SHAKE all ingredients with ice and fine strain into chilled glass.

name: Ante
ingredients: ½ shot De Kuyper Triple Sec Liqueur
instructions: STIR all ingredients with ice and strain into chilled glass.
comment: Medium dry, complex spiced apple with hints of orange.

name: Amaretto Sour
instructions: DRY SHAKE (without ice) all ingredients to emulsify. Add ice, SHAKE again and strain into ice-filled glass.

name: Army & Navy

name: Bajan Daiquiri

name: Bermuda Cocktail

name: Black Feather

name: Bonnie Prince Charles

name: Caribe Daiquiri

name: Deauville #1

name: Dirty Sanchez

name: Divino's

name: El Burro

name: Apple & Melon Cocktail
ingredients: ½ shot Freshly squeezed lime juice

name: El Diablo Cocktail
ingredients: 2 shot Patrón Reposado (orange seal) 100% agave tequila

Top up with Three Cents Ginger Beer
instructions: SHAKE first 3 ingredients with ice and strain into ice-filled glass. TOP with ginger beer.
comment:
The tequila, red fruit and ginger aren't exactly a subtle combination but it is one that has proved both popular and enduring.

UK bartenders and other countries where 25ml and 50ml measures are standard may prefer this recipe:

50ml Tequila reposado
20ml Crème de cassis
25ml Freshly squeezed lime juice
Top with Ginger beer
